subject: common-octopus
gloss: The common octopus is a marine mollusk.
excerpt: The common octopus (Octopus vulgaris) is a marine mollusk belonging to the class Cephalopoda.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: The common octopus belongs to the class Cephalopoda.
excerpt: The common octopus (Octopus vulgaris) is a marine mollusk belonging to the class Cephalopoda.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: The scientific name of the common octopus is Octopus vulgaris.
excerpt: The common octopus (Octopus vulgaris) is a marine mollusk belonging to the class Cephalopoda.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: The common octopus is found in the Mediterranean Sea.
excerpt: It is found in tropical and temperate waters worldwide, particularly in the Mediterranean Sea and the eastern Atlantic Ocean.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: The common octopus is found in the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
excerpt: It is found in tropical and temperate waters worldwide, particularly in the Mediterranean Sea and the eastern Atlantic Ocean.

subject: common-octopus
excerpt: Octopuses have three hearts: two branchial hearts that pump blood through the gills, and one systemic heart that pumps blood through the body.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: Octopus blood is blue due to copper-based hemocyanin.
excerpt: Their blood is blue because it contains copper-based hemocyanin rather than iron-based hemoglobin.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: An adult common octopus typically weighs between 3 and 5 kilograms.
excerpt: An adult common octopus typically weighs between 3 and 5 kilograms and has an arm span of up to 1 meter.

subject: common-octopus
excerpt: They have approximately 500 million neurons, with about two-thirds located in their arms rather than their central brain.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: The octopus is a master of camouflage using chromatophores.
excerpt: The octopus is a master of camouflage.

subject: chromatophores
gloss: Chromatophores allow octopuses to change color and texture in less than a second.
excerpt: Specialized skin cells called chromatophores allow them to change color and texture in less than a second.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: Moray eels are a primary predator of octopuses.
excerpt: Their primary predators include moray eels, sharks, and dolphins.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: Sharks are a primary predator of octopuses.
excerpt: Their primary predators include moray eels, sharks, and dolphins.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: Octopuses have a short lifespan of 1 to 2 years.
excerpt: Octopuses are solitary creatures with a short lifespan of 1 to 2 years.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: The octopus reproductive strategy is known as semelparity.
excerpt: This reproductive strategy is known as semelparity.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: The common octopus primarily feeds on crabs.
excerpt: The common octopus primarily feeds on crabs, crayfish, and bivalve mollusks.

subject: common-octopus
gloss: Each octopus arm has approximately 240 suckers.
excerpt: Each arm has approximately 240 suckers that can taste and grip independently.

subject: university-of-otago
gloss: The University of Otago is in New Zealand.
excerpt: In laboratory experiments at the University of Otago in New Zealand, octopuses demonstrated the ability to recognize individual human faces.
